rows.name: variant
rows.description: Visual treatment for the action. Use `destructive` only for high-risk actions.

rows.name: disabled
rows.description: Makes the control inert. In link mode, `fd-button` uses `aria-disabled="true"` and suppresses navigation.

rows.description: When set, `fd-button` renders a native `<a>` instead of an internal `<button>`.

rows.description: Native link relationship tokens. When `target="_blank"`, `fd-button` always adds `noopener noreferrer`.

rows.name: loading
rows.description: Shows a spinner and makes the control inert while work is in progress.

rows.name: loading-label
rows.description: Optional replacement label to show and announce while `loading` is true.

properties.after: `fd-button` currently exposes a reactive `type` property in source, but v1 always renders `<button type="button">` in button mode. Submit/reset behavior is not part of the supported public contract.

shadowParts.after:
- **Primary** carries the main action on the page or section. Limit it to one primary button per section.
- **Destructive** is for irreversible actions like deleting records or revoking access. Pair it with a confirmation step.
- **Icon-only** buttons require an accessible name on `fd-button`, such as `aria-label`.
- **Icon-only** buttons render as square controls with the icon centered horizontally and vertically.
- **Link mode** uses `href` and renders a native `<a>`, preserving link semantics.
- **Loading** prevents duplicate activation while the action is in progress. Use `loading-label` when the wait may be noticeable.

shadowParts.after:
**Migration note:** In previous versions, `::part(base)` targeted the native `<input>` directly. It now targets the visual container `<div>`. For border, background, and radius customization, `::part(base)` continues to work as before.

`::part()` cannot chain with pseudo-elements like `::placeholder`. Use the documented CSS custom properties instead when you need to style placeholder text or slot sizing.

### Icon sizing

Icons inside prefix/suffix slots are automatically sized to 22px via `--fd-input-icon-size`. This is derived from the input's 18px body text at a 1.25× scale factor, rounded down to the nearest multiple of 2 (18 × 1.25 = 22.5 → 22px). The 1.25× ratio produces a glyph that is visually proportional to the accompanying text.

You do not need to set `--fd-icon-size` on slotted icons or buttons — the slot container sets it automatically.

**General principle:** Icons should be sized in proportion to the text they are paired with. When an icon appears inline with or adjacent to a text-bearing control, size the icon at 1.25× the text's font size, rounded down to the nearest multiple of 2px. This keeps the glyph visually balanced with the text baseline and line height.

To override for a specific input, set `--fd-input-icon-size`:

```html
<fd-input style="--fd-input-icon-size: 24px;">
  <fd-icon slot="prefix" name="magnifying-glass" aria-hidden="true"></fd-icon>
</fd-input>
```

properties.after:
- `items` is a property-only API. Set it from JavaScript rather than authoring it as an HTML attribute.
- Accepted files remain the source of truth for form submission, `required`, and `maxFiles` behavior even when `items` is provided.

slots.after: This component does not use slots. Configure field copy and file rows through its public properties.

properties.after:
- v1 is intentionally limited to single-value integer selection.
- Invalid authored numeric constraints are normalized predictably, with console warnings for authoring mistakes.

slots.after: This component does not use slots. Configure its content through public properties.

rows.name: input
rows.description: Fired when the committed value changes through drag, keyboard input, or valid inline-input edits.

rows.name: change
rows.description: Fired when a value change is committed by the range input or the inline helper input.

rows.description: Visual rail height.

rows.name: --fd-slider-input-radius

rows.name: --fd-slider-input-background
rows.description: Inline helper input background color.

rows.name: label
rows.description: Visible field label.

rows.name: hint

rows.name: control
rows.description: Wrapper that groups the range input and optional helper input.

rows.description: Visual unfilled rail under the native range input.

rows.name: fill
rows.description: Visual filled rail up to the committed value.

rows.name: range

rows.name: value-bubble

rows.name: input

rows.name: label
rows.description: Adds `role="group"` and `aria-label` when the chip set benefits from a shared accessible name.

rows.name: default
rows.description: One or more `fd-chip` elements.

rows.name: --fd-chip-group-gap
rows.description: Gap between chips in the wrapping layout.

rows.name: container
rows.description: Internal wrapping flex container.

rows.name: name
rows.description: Submitted form field name.

rows.name: value
rows.description: Current textarea value.

rows.name: placeholder
rows.description: Optional placeholder text. Use for examples, not as the visible label.

rows.name: disabled
rows.description: Prevents editing and submission.

rows.name: readonly
rows.description: Prevents editing while keeping the value focusable and selectable.

rows.name: required
rows.description: Marks the field as required for constraint validation.

rows.name: rows
rows.description: Initial visible row count for the native textarea.

rows.name: maxlength
rows.description: Maximum character count. Also enables the built-in character counter.

rows.name: minlength
rows.description: Minimum length requirement passed through to the native textarea.

rows.name: autocomplete
rows.description: Native autocomplete hint for longer-text fields.

rows.name: input
rows.description: Fired on each value change.

rows.name: change
rows.description: Fired when the native textarea commits a value change.

rows.name: --fd-textarea-min-height
rows.description: Minimum textarea height.

rows.name: --fd-textarea-border-color
rows.description: Border color at rest.

rows.name: --fd-textarea-border-color-hover
rows.description: Border color on hover.

rows.name: --fd-textarea-border-color-focus
rows.description: Focus glow color.

rows.name: --fd-textarea-border-radius
rows.description: Corner radius.

rows.name: --fd-textarea-bg
rows.description: Background color.

rows.name: --fd-textarea-placeholder-color
rows.description: Placeholder text color.

rows.name: base
rows.description: Visual textarea container (`<div>`) for border, background, radius, focus, and state styling.

rows.name: native
rows.description: The native `<textarea>` element. Exposed for JavaScript access such as `el.shadowRoot.querySelector('[part=native]')`.

rows.name: wrapper
rows.description: Outermost `<div>` containing the textarea container and character count.

rows.name: char-count
rows.description: Visible character count display.

shadowParts.after:
`fd-textarea` keeps native manual resize enabled in the vertical direction only. Width remains controlled by the component container.

When `maxlength` is present, the visible count is always shown and the screen-reader live region follows the same threshold-announcement contract as `fd-input`.

rows.name: type
rows.description: Visual treatment for the circular surface. Use `avatar` when the visual should clip avatar media or render the placeholder avatar fallback.

rows.name: size
rows.description: Applies one of the fixed Figma size presets for the overall circle and its inner content.

rows.name: default
rows.description: Optional decorative icon or avatar media. Leave empty to use the built-in fallback glyph or avatar placeholder.

slots.after: Slot content is decorative in v1. Do not slot interactive controls or content that must be announced independently.

rows.name: --fd-visual-size
rows.description: Overrides the rendered circle size.

rows.name: --fd-visual-padding
rows.description: Overrides the inner padding for non-avatar variants.

rows.name: --fd-visual-content-size
rows.description: Overrides the inner content box size for non-avatar variants.

rows.name: --fd-visual-radius
rows.description: Corner radius for the circular surface.

rows.name: --fd-visual-bg-neutral
rows.description: Neutral surface background.

rows.name: --fd-visual-fg-neutral
rows.description: Neutral foreground color used by the fallback glyph and slotted icon content.

rows.name: --fd-visual-bg-cool
rows.description: Cool surface background.

rows.name: --fd-visual-fg-cool
rows.description: Cool foreground color used by the fallback glyph and slotted icon content.

rows.name: --fd-visual-bg-warm
rows.description: Warm surface background.

rows.name: --fd-visual-fg-warm
rows.description: Warm foreground color used by the fallback glyph and slotted icon content.

rows.name: --fd-visual-bg-avatar
rows.description: Avatar background surface.

rows.name: --fd-visual-avatar-placeholder-color
rows.description: Placeholder avatar silhouette color.

rows.name: surface
rows.description: Outer circular wrapper.

rows.name: content
rows.description: Inner frame that centers slotted media or the fallback visual.

rows.name: fallback
rows.description: Built-in archive glyph or placeholder avatar wrapper when no slot content is supplied.

shadowParts.after:
- `fd-visual` stays decorative-only in v1 and sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the host.
- For avatar images, prefer empty `alt` text when adjacent content already names the subject.

rows.name: variant
rows.description: Controls the supplied Figma layout family: standard stacked alert, compact slim alert, or page-level site alert.

rows.name: type
rows.description: Severity treatment for the alert surface, icon, and color system.

rows.name: title
rows.description: Optional plain-text title rendered by the component. Slim alerts usually omit it, but the property is available across variants.

rows.name: dismissible
rows.description: Shows the internal dismiss button. The component emits an event but does not remove itself.

rows.name: dismiss-label
rows.description: Optional accessible-name override for the dismiss button. Defaults to `Dismiss {title}` or `Dismiss alert`.

rows.name: live
rows.description: Controls whether the alert stays static, announces politely via `role="status"`, or announces assertively via `role="alert"`.

properties.after:
- `variant="site"` keeps its page-level region semantics even when `live` is enabled by nesting the live-region wrapper inside the site banner.
- `type="emergency"` is supported for all variants, including `site`; the site emergency styling is a small documented inference from the supplied Figma family.

rows.name: default
rows.description: Authored alert body content. Inline links are supported; dedicated CTA rows are intentionally out of scope in v1.

slots.after: Use the default slot for visible message copy. The component does not expose a separate actions slot in v1.

rows.name: fd-alert-dismiss
rows.description: Fired when the internal dismiss button is activated.

events.after: `fd-alert` does not hide or remove itself. The host application owns dismissal state and any follow-up focus recovery after removal.

rows.name: --fd-alert-radius
rows.description: Outer corner radius for the alert surface.

rows.name: --fd-alert-accent-width
rows.description: Inline accent width for `default` and `slim` alerts.

rows.name: --fd-alert-site-accent-width
rows.description: Top accent width for `site` alerts.

rows.name: --fd-alert-icon-size
rows.description: Icon size for `default` and `slim` variants.

rows.name: --fd-alert-site-icon-size
rows.description: Icon size for the `site` variant.

rows.name: --fd-alert-focus-ring
rows.description: Focus-ring color for links and the dismiss button.

rows.name: --fd-alert-focus-gap
rows.description: Inner gap color used in the dismiss-button focus ring.

rows.name: --fd-alert-bg-info
rows.description: Info alert background.

rows.name: --fd-alert-accent-info
rows.description: Info alert accent and icon color.

rows.name: --fd-alert-bg-success
rows.description: Success alert background.

rows.name: --fd-alert-accent-success
rows.description: Success alert accent and icon color.

rows.name: --fd-alert-bg-warning
rows.description: Warning alert background.

rows.name: --fd-alert-accent-warning
rows.description: Warning alert accent and icon color.

rows.name: --fd-alert-bg-error
rows.description: Error alert background.

rows.name: --fd-alert-accent-error
rows.description: Error alert accent and icon color.

rows.name: --fd-alert-bg-emergency
rows.description: Emergency alert background.

rows.name: --fd-alert-accent-emergency
rows.description: Emergency alert accent.

rows.name: --fd-alert-text-color
rows.description: Default alert text color.

rows.name: --fd-alert-text-color-inverted
rows.description: Text color used for emergency alerts.

rows.name: --fd-alert-link-color
rows.description: Link color for non-emergency alerts.

rows.name: --fd-alert-link-color-inverted
rows.description: Link color for emergency alerts.

rows.name: base
rows.description: Outer alert surface. For `site`, this is the named page-level section wrapper.

rows.name: icon
rows.description: Decorative severity icon wrapper.

rows.name: title
rows.description: Rendered title text when the `title` property is set.

rows.name: body
rows.description: Visible body-content wrapper around the default slot.

rows.name: dismiss-button
rows.description: Internal native dismiss button.

rows.name: dismiss-icon
rows.description: Decorative close icon wrapper inside the dismiss button.

shadowParts.after:
- `fd-alert` keeps the severity icon decorative and adds a screen-reader-only severity label in the rendered content.
- The component does not expose a shadow part for the live-region wrapper because that wrapper is an implementation detail of the semantic contract, not a styling surface.

rows.name: type
rows.description: Visual tone for the decorative stripe.

slots.after: `fd-stripe` intentionally exposes no slots in v1. The component owns the decorative line rendering directly.

rows.name: --fd-stripe-width
rows.description: Rendered stripe width. The component clamps to the available container width.

rows.name: --fd-stripe-height
rows.description: Rendered stripe thickness.

rows.name: --fd-stripe-radius
rows.description: Corner radius for the stripe.

rows.name: --fd-stripe-bg-neutral
rows.description: Neutral stripe color.

rows.name: --fd-stripe-bg-cool
rows.description: Cool stripe color.

rows.name: --fd-stripe-bg-warm
rows.description: Warm stripe color.

rows.name: stripe
rows.description: Internal decorative line element.

shadowParts.after:
- `fd-stripe` stays decorative-only in v1 and sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the host.
- Use surrounding headings or landmarks for structure; the stripe itself is not a semantic separator.

rows.name: href
rows.description: Link destination. Supported usage should provide `href` so the component remains a real hyperlink.

rows.name: target
rows.description: Native link target.

rows.name: rel
rows.description: Native link relationship tokens. When `target="_blank"`, `fd-link` always adds `noopener noreferrer`.

rows.name: variant
rows.description: Visual treatment for the hyperlink.

rows.name: size
rows.description: Typography scale for the hyperlink.

properties.after:
- `fd-link` forwards `aria-label`, `aria-labelledby`, and `aria-current` from the host to the internal anchor.
- The `visited` variant is an explicit visual treatment in v1. It does not inspect browser history.
- `subtle` starts without an underline and gains the emphasized underline treatment on hover and focus.

rows.name: default
rows.description: Authored link text.

rows.name: --fd-link-color-normal
rows.description: Default text color for the normal variant.

rows.name: --fd-link-color-visited
rows.description: Text color for the visited variant.

rows.name: --fd-link-color-subtle
rows.description: Default text color for the subtle variant before hover or focus.

rows.name: --fd-link-color-inverted
rows.description: Text color for links on dark surfaces.

rows.name: --fd-link-hover-overlay
rows.description: Background overlay used for hover and focus emphasis.

rows.name: --fd-link-focus-gap
rows.description: Inner gap color in the focus ring.

rows.name: --fd-link-focus-ring
rows.description: Outer focus-ring color.

rows.name: --fd-link-underline-thickness
rows.description: Default underline thickness.

rows.name: --fd-link-underline-thickness-emphasis
rows.description: Underline thickness on hover and focus.

rows.name: --fd-link-underline-offset
rows.description: Underline offset from the text baseline.

rows.name: base
rows.description: Internal native `<a>` element.

rows.name: current-page
rows.description: Current page in the bounded set. Values below `1` normalize to `1`; values above `total-pages` clamp to the last page for rendering.

rows.name: total-pages
rows.description: Total number of pages in the bounded set. V1 intentionally requires a known last page.

rows.name: href-template
rows.description: Optional href template for link mode. Use a `{page}` token, for example `/results?page={page}`.

properties.after:
- Set `aria-label` on `fd-pagination` when more than one pagination landmark appears on the page.
- Without `href-template`, the component stays action-based and emits `fd-page-request` for the application to handle.

slots.after: No slots are exposed in v1.

rows.name: fd-page-request
rows.description: Fired when the user requests another page from a numbered control, previous/next control, or the mobile page select.

events.after: Cancel `fd-page-request` to intercept link-mode navigation, including the mobile select behavior when `href-template` is present.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-gap
rows.description: Gap between previous/next controls and the desktop page list.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-page-gap
rows.description: Gap between numbered desktop page items.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-mobile-gap
rows.description: Gap between controls in the mobile layout.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-control-min-size
rows.description: Minimum touch target size for pagination controls.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-radius
rows.description: Corner radius for controls and the mobile page select.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-control-bg
rows.description: Background for non-current page controls.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-control-color
rows.description: Text color for non-current page controls and the mobile summary.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-control-bg-disabled
rows.description: Background for disabled previous/next controls.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-control-color-disabled
rows.description: Text color for disabled controls and ellipsis items.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-current-bg
rows.description: Background for the current page control.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-current-color
rows.description: Text color for the current page control.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-select-bg
rows.description: Background for the mobile page select.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-select-border
rows.description: Border color for the mobile page select.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-focus-gap
rows.description: Inner gap color in the focus ring.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-focus-ring
rows.description: Outer focus ring color.

rows.name: --fd-pagination-collapse-at
rows.description: Width threshold below which the component switches to the mobile layout.

rows.name: nav
rows.description: Internal pagination landmark.

rows.name: list
rows.description: Desktop unordered list of numbered page controls.

rows.name: item
rows.description: Desktop list item wrapper for a page control or ellipsis.

rows.name: control
rows.description: Internal previous, next, or page control.

rows.name: ellipsis
rows.description: Ellipsis marker used when page ranges are omitted.

rows.name: mobile-select
rows.description: Mobile native page select.

rows.name: mobile-summary
rows.description: Mobile `of N` page-count summary.
